decision_summary: Bounded browser-side Supabase auth storage is permitted for PKCE verifier continuity, Supabase session recognition after callback exchange, and protected-shell session guard evaluation only. This is not application data persistence and does not authorize backend, database, tenant, customer workspace, audit-ledger, billing, or production SaaS persistence.

supabase_docs.summary: Supabase PKCE callback exchange requires both the returned auth code and a locally stored code verifier; the exchange must happen on the same browser/device that started the flow.

Supabase JavaScript auth defaults to persistSession true and stores the session in local storage when available; when storage is unavailable, a custom storage implementation is required or persistSession can be disabled.
getSession reads the session from the storage attached to the client and may return null when no session is detected.

package_behavior_inference: The PKCE code verifier must be recoverable by the callback exchange client after OAuth redirect.
A non-persistent in-memory storage boundary is insufficient for full-page OAuth redirect continuity.
Supabase-managed browser auth storage is the smallest supported browser-side continuity mechanism for both the verifier and the exchanged session.

purpose: PKCE code verifier continuity across provider redirect
Supabase auth session recognition after exchangeCodeForSession(code)
protected-shell session guard evaluation through Supabase auth APIs
permitted_storage_behavior: PKCE verifier persistence only as managed by Supabase auth internals
Supabase auth session persistence until explicit sign-out, Supabase/session expiry, browser storage clearing, or equivalent Supabase-auth lifecycle end

selected_future_runtime_candidate.rationale: The smallest later implementation candidate is to enable Supabase-managed browser auth persistence for PKCE verifier and session continuity while preserving the existing manual query-code exchange path and keeping background auto-refresh disabled unless separately justified.

alternatives_rejected_or_deferred.reason: It preserves the previous no-persistence boundary but blocks real Supabase PKCE callback exchange continuity.
It is narrower than the documented Supabase auth client storage model and would not truthfully cover protected-shell getSession continuity after callback.
The permitted boundary is now explicitly scoped to Supabase auth state only and excludes application data persistence.
No service-role key, JWT secret, backend storage, tenant persistence, customer data, billing, or regulated application-data persistence is authorized by this decision.
The current callback path already reads the query code and calls exchangeCodeForSession(code); automatic URL detection is not the smallest necessary implementation candidate.
Initial callback and protected-shell continuity can be decided without authorizing background refresh; enablement would require a separate lifecycle decision.
Default Supabase browser storage is smaller than adding repository-owned storage adapter code.

claimable_after_this_pass: Bounded Supabase-managed browser auth storage is permitted as a future implementation boundary for PKCE verifier continuity and Supabase session guard continuity only.

The decision does not implement runtime auth storage behavior, live OAuth verification, callback/session recognition verification, protected-shell admission verification, backend/database persistence, or Authenticated Surfaces birth.

next_threshold.name: §1.2 Supabase Auth Storage Implementation 0.1 — Bounded PKCE/Auth Session Continuity
next_threshold.purpose: Implement only the approved Supabase-managed auth storage continuity boundary, then separately rerun live callback/session recognition and protected-shell admission verification without claiming Authenticated Surfaces birth until those live checks pass.
must_not_implement: custom storage adapter unless separately authorized
